_comment: Mononuclear heteroelement oxoacid table - Stage 6 R1-B.
Maps RDKit-canonical SMILES of standalone (HO)_n X(=O)_m acids / salts to
the OPSIN-accepted preferred IUPAC name.  Keys are RDKit canonical SMILES
(obtained via Chem.MolToSmiles after parsing the OPSIN reference SMILES
from nonCarboxylicAcids.xml).  Values are the single IUPAC name for that
standalone molecule; the engine looks up via whole-molecule canonical
Source: OPSIN 2.8.0 nonCarboxylicAcids.xml.  Every entry has been
probed with py2opsin for round-trip (name -> SMILES -> same canonical)
perrhenous / perruthenous acid) and 'sulfinamous acid' are the
traditional lower-oxidation-state forms registered in OPSIN; each is
the structural complement of the corresponding '-ic' entry above (one
fewer terminal =O on the central atom), keyed on its own distinct
RDKit-canonical SMILES.  source: opsin (P-67 / IR-8 traditional
inorganic acid names) -- NOT phase pins; the data entry is the
_subsumed_note: tellurate/tellurite; boric; all halogen oxyacid acids and anions;
phosphonic/phosphinic/arsinic) were REMOVED because the generative
maingroup_oxoacids.compute_name reproduces every one of them exactly
from structural features (verified by round-trip).  Only entries the
generator does NOT cover remain below: d-block metal acids (Cr/Mn/
Tc/Re/Ru — outside the main-group scope), the Sb 'stibonite' anion
(pnictogen anions are out of the generator's anion scope), and
